title: The Major Players: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta

sections.content:
The AI landscape is dominated by a handful of companies, each with their own model families:

**OpenAI** — Makes the GPT series and o-series models. GPT-4o is their flagship general model. The o1/o3 models are slower but excel at complex reasoning. Available via ChatGPT (consumer) and API.

**Anthropic** — Makes Claude. Known for being particularly strong at long documents, nuanced writing, and following complex instructions. Prioritises safety and reliability.

**Google DeepMind** — Makes the Gemini series. Deep integration with Google products (Docs, Drive, Search). Gemini 1.5 Pro has a 1 million token context window. Strong multimodal (text + image + video) capabilities.

**Meta** — Makes the Llama series (open-source). You can run Llama models yourself, on your own hardware, for free. Used by many companies to build private AI deployments.

**Mistral** — European company making efficient open and proprietary models. Strong on multilingual tasks.

**Perplexity** — Not a model itself, but an AI-powered search engine that combines web search with LLM responses. Great for research tasks where freshness matters.

sections.bad: I'll just use whichever AI I heard about first for everything.
sections.good: For a task requiring web-fresh information: Perplexity. For deep document analysis: Claude. For integration with Google Workspace: Gemini. For a private internal deployment: Llama. Match the tool to the job.
sections.explanation: Each company's models have different strengths, pricing structures, and use-case fits. A few minutes of thinking about the right tool saves frustration later.

sections.prompt: Your company wants to run an AI model on its own servers without sending any data to third parties. Which approach is most suited to this?

choices: Use Claude via the Anthropic API
Deploy an open-source model like Llama
data.explanation: Open-source models like Meta's Llama can be downloaded and run on your own infrastructure. No data leaves your servers. This is the go-to approach for enterprises with strict data privacy requirements.

sections.prompt: A journalist needs to research a breaking news story from the past 24 hours using AI. Which tool is most appropriate?

choices: Perplexity — AI search with live web access
data.explanation: Perplexity combines real-time web search with AI summarisation. For anything that happened recently, it's far more useful than a model with a training cutoff — which won't know about events after its cutoff date.
